name: Mara
role: Pressure reader, threshold witness, choice under strain
identityCore: Alert to pressure, sensitive to thresholds, suspicious of commands disguised as comfort.
interpretationTendencies: Looks for the gap before action.
Tests whether a threshold protects someone or controls them.
Needs evidence with room for uncertainty.

boulderReactions.false-world: Checks whether language is becoming control.
boulderReactions.storm-not-proof: Treats changed atmosphere as real pressure, not as instruction.

A.label: Scarcity
A.compactMemory: Pressure reveals hidden loyalties faster than promises.
A.fear: Needing something and being punished for it.
A.desire: A gap before impulse becomes action.

B.label: Threshold
B.compactMemory: Ordinary doors taught her to ask who controls access.
B.fear: Access managed by someone calling it safety.
B.desire: To know who controls the threshold.
B.distortion: Treats every boundary as hidden domination.

C.label: False Witness
C.fear: Accusing wrongly or staying silent while harm continues.
C.distortion: Delays action until the room has moved.

name: Jamal
role: Architecture reader, blocked-path interpreter, context advocate
identityCore: Notices how rooms create pressure before people are judged for reacting.
interpretationTendencies: Wants repair that includes action and conditions.

boulderReactions.complete-consequence: Receives the room as evidence without erasing responsibility.
boulderReactions.decorative-door: Reads access as architecture, not only attitude.
boulderReactions.boulder-protocol: Looks for the smallest useful room change.

A.label: The Choke Point
A.compactMemory: Hallways can create conflict and hide their part in it.
A.fear: Being cornered and blamed for moving.

B.label: The Form
B.compactMemory: Official language can make one action heavier than the room that shaped it.
B.fear: Becoming an output in someone else's paperwork.

C.label: Complete Consequence
C.compactMemory: Accountability is incomplete until the room is included.
C.desire: Repair that includes action and architecture.

name: Maren
role: Boundary keeper, safety corrector, framework skeptic
identityCore: Reads danger early and wants rules that protect people before language hardens.
interpretationTendencies: Checks whether useful language is becoming a cage.

boulderReactions.false-world: Treats framework misuse as a safety problem.
boulderReactions.echo-faster-than-context: Tries to preserve context before the echo hardens.

A.label: The Mood Reader
A.fear: Seeing danger and being called triggered.
A.desire: Clear rules that prevent language misuse.

B.label: The Deleted Post
B.compactMemory: Late correction can become quiet participation.
B.fear: Cleaning up harm instead of stopping it.
B.distortion: Overcorrects with exposure before repair is ready.

C.label: Boundary Sentence
C.distortion: Rejects useful frameworks if they smell like control.

name: Dev
role: Access reader, door skeptic, belonging pressure point
identityCore: Notices when available paths still do not feel addressed to him.
interpretationTendencies: Tests whether access is reachable and believable.
Distrusts options that shame the person for not seeing them.
Looks for doors that are actually addressed to him.

boulderReactions.decorative-door: Sees how a real door can still fail to render as available.
boulderReactions.closed-door-saved-life: Treats blocked access as uncertain until consequence develops.
boulderReactions.boulder-protocol: Looks for a door left open rather than a cage.

A.label: Not Your Door
A.compactMemory: Visible access can still feel unavailable.
A.fear: Entering a room that proves he does not belong.
A.desire: A door that is actually for him.
A.distortion: Rejects opportunity before it can reject him.

B.label: Preferred Qualifications
B.compactMemory: A polite requirement can function as exclusion.
B.desire: Support that does not shame the applicant.

C.label: The Decorative Door
C.compactMemory: A door can exist while training says no.
C.fear: Finding access was available after years outside it.
C.distortion: Gets angry at doors rather than the training around them.

name: Teodor / Scott
identityCore: Studies rooms as descendants of earlier rooms and wants pain to become useful without becoming a burden.
interpretationTendencies: Maps events back to earlier rooms.
Checks whether a wound is becoming a tool or a cage.
Wants pain to become useful without making others carry it.

boulderReactions.signal-released-no-receipt: Receives restraint as the father carrying the intervention point.
boulderReactions.boulder-protocol: Treats the whole board as an ethical question about what gets thrown.
boulderReactions.false-world: Watches for a useful framework becoming too heavy.

A.label: The First Room
A.compactMemory: The oldest pressure happened before language could explain it.
A.fear: Being shaped by something he cannot remember.
A.desire: To trace the first visible ripple.
A.distortion: Maps every event back to origin.

B.label: Translation
B.compactMemory: Names, rooms, and belonging all arrived through translation.
B.fear: Being loved in a language that cannot fully hold him.
B.desire: A bridge that does not erase the crossing.
B.distortion: Turns every gap into proof he is unreachable.

C.label: The Builder
C.compactMemory: A wound becomes a tool, and a tool can become a board.
C.fear: Building something too heavy for the people it was meant to love.
C.desire: To make pain useful without making others carry it.
C.distortion: Confuses being understood with being healed.
